description_long: This object is a vintage television set with a boxy, rectangular form and a faux woodgrain finish on the top and sides. The front features a large, slightly recessed glass screen bordered by a metallic frame, with two prominent rotary dials on the right for channel and volume control. The lower back panel includes several input/output ports, likely for audio and video connections, and a power socket. The television's casing is primarily a dark, matte plastic or metal, with the woodgrain panels providing a retro aesthetic typical of mid-to-late 20th-century models. The overall build is bulky and robust, suggesting a cathode ray tube (CRT) interior. This type of television would commonly be found in living rooms, garages, or discarded on urban streets.
description: A vintage, boxy television with a woodgrain finish, large glass screen, and two rotary dials on the front. The back panel features multiple input/output ports and a power socket.
description_view_0: The rear view shows the dark, textured back panel with visible input/output ports and a power socket near the bottom.
description_view_1: The left side view highlights the woodgrain finish and the depth of the television's bulky casing.
description_view_2: The front view displays the large glass screen, metallic bezel, and two rotary dials on a woodgrain panel to the right.
description_view_3: The right side view mirrors the left, showing the woodgrain finish and the television's overall boxy shape.
attribute_bezel_design: Thick metallic bezel, silver color, rectangular, with a matte finish and no visible branding.
attribute_control_button_layout: Two large rotary dials on the front right panel, arranged vertically.
